fields.caption: you can't be mad about paying $5/M for Opus / if Sonnet 5 already closes the gap for $2/M

narrative_summary: The joke is that Opus 4.8 buyers outsmarted themselves — Roll Safe logic applied to the real price gap between Sonnet 5 ($2/M) and Opus ($5/M) with benchmarks nearly identical.
rationale: Candidate 3 earns its punchline with real numbers ($5/M vs $2/M) and a genuine insight — Opus buyers got quietly undercut by a cheaper model closing the benchmark gap — which is exactly the Roll Safe tautological-logic shape. The specificity does the comedic heavy lifting that the other candidates leave vague.
place: at a congressional hearing about AI
hat_pair: hard hat covered entirely in sticky notes reading URGENT
